expectations.text: Housing is identified as the largest expense category ($2,200)
expectations.evidence: Housing listed first at $2,200.00 with note 'Housing is by far the largest at $2,200'

expectations.text: Spending is broken down by account (Chase Checking vs Amex Gold)
expectations.evidence: Account Split section lists Chase Checking and Amex Gold transactions, though without dollar totals per account

expectations.text: At least one transaction is flagged as notable or unusual
expectations.evidence: 'The $2,200 rent payment stands out as much larger than any other single expense'

expectations.text: Categories are ranked or sorted by total spend amount
expectations.evidence: Categories numbered 1-9 in descending order by amount

expectations.text: Missing values or data quality issues are mentioned (even if none found)
expectations.evidence: No mention of missing values, data quality, or data completeness anywhere in the output

suggestions.assertion: Spending is broken down by account (Chase Checking vs Amex Gold)
suggestions.reason: This passed despite not including dollar totals per account. Consider requiring numeric totals per account, not just listing which transactions go to which account.

eval_feedback.overall: The data quality assertion effectively discriminates - without the skill's patterns, executors don't think to check for missing values.
